data.title: Bitdeer Plans $300 Million Convertible Note Offering
data.description:
Bitdeer announced on February 19 its intention to issue $300 million in convertible senior notes due in 2032 through a private placement. According to BlockBeats, the company also plans to grant initial purchasers an option to buy an additional $45 million.

The net proceeds from this offering will be partially used to cover the cost of capped call transactions and to repurchase some of the 5.25% convertible bonds due in 2029. The remaining funds will primarily support data center expansion, high-performance computing, AI cloud business growth, ASIC miner research and manufacturing, as well as general corporate purposes.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | U.S. President Trump to Host G20 Summit in Miami This December
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump is set to host the G20 Summit in Miami from December 14 to 15, according to Jin10. The U.S. Treasury Department announced that the summit will bring together leaders from the world's major economies to discuss global economic issues. Additionally, the U.S. will host G20 finance officials for meetings in Asheville, North Carolina, in late August and September. These gatherings aim to address financial and economic challenges facing the international community.

data.title: Digital Currency Revolutionizes U.S. Financial Landscape
data.description: A new form of digital currency is transforming the way Americans manage and transfer their dollars. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Wall Street's swift efforts to capitalize on this emerging trend. As digital money gains traction, financial institutions are eager to integrate these innovations into their operations, aiming to enhance efficiency and meet evolving consumer demands. This shift signifies a significant change in the financial sector, with potential implications for traditional banking systems and the broader economy. The rise of digital currency is prompting a reevaluation of existing financial practices, as stakeholders seek to adapt to this rapidly changing environment.

data.title: Voltage Launches Credit Line for Instant Bitcoin Payments Settled in USD
data.description: Voltage has launched Voltage Credit, a revolving credit line designed to enable businesses to make instant payments using Bitcoin's infrastructure, with settlements in U.S. dollars. According to NS3.AI, this service allows companies to avoid holding cryptocurrency on their balance sheets and eliminates the need for pre-funding. It offers fast finality and low fees through Bitcoin's Lightning Network and on-chain transactions. The product is aimed at both crypto-native and traditional companies looking for efficient payment solutions without the risk of cryptocurrency volatility.

data.title: Prices of many traditional financial assets have rebounded, and volatility indices have risen across the board.
data.description: According to the latest data from Gate, gold prices rose to $4,977.5 per ounce, a daily increase of 0.90%; silver prices rose to $78.46 per ounce, a daily increase of 2.51%. Regarding volatility indices, the BVIX (BTC volatility index) is currently at 54.64, up 5.34% on the day; the EVIX (ETH volatility index) is currently at 69.51, up 1.53% on the day. In commodities, WTI crude oil rose 4.07% to $66.27 per barrel; Brent crude oil rose 3.93% to $71.72 per barrel. Gate allows users to directly trade traditional financial market products on its platform, providing a one-stop service covering precious metals, forex, global equity CFDs, major indices, and commodities, achieving deep integration of crypto assets and traditional financial assets. Gate TradFi features have been fully integrated into the Gate App and Web platform, allowing users to easily participate in global asset price trading without switching platforms, unlocking more strategies and opportunities outside the crypto market, and continuously enhancing their diversified asset allocation experience.

data.title: South Africa Grants Emergency Water License to Address Shortages
data.description: South Africa's government has issued an urgent license to the continent's largest bulk-water supplier, allowing it to extract additional water from a crucial river system. Bloomberg posted on X, this decision aims to stabilize water supplies in the country's wealthiest province, which is currently facing shortages. The move underscores the pressing need to address water scarcity issues affecting the region. Authorities are taking steps to ensure that the increased extraction does not adversely impact the river system's sustainability. This development highlights the ongoing challenges in managing water resources in South Africa.

data.title: Sam Bankman-Fried's Conviction Overturned by Appeals Court
data.description: The U.S. Court of Appeals for the Second Circuit has overturned the conviction of Sam Bankman-Fried, founder of FTX. According to NS3.AI, the appeals court determined that the trial judge had improperly excluded evidence that was vital to Bankman-Fried's defense. Bankman-Fried expressed approval of the decision, emphasizing that the evidence demonstrated FTX's business practices were consistent with industry standards and had received legal approvals.

data.title: Renault CEO Highlights AI's Role in Cost Reduction and Quality Improvement
data.description: Renault CEO Francois Provost has emphasized the significant impact of artificial intelligence (AI) on the automotive industry. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Provost's remarks that AI is not only aiding in cost reduction but also enhancing manufacturing processes and product quality. The integration of AI technologies is seen as a pivotal factor in driving efficiency and innovation within the company. Provost's comments underscore the growing importance of AI in transforming traditional manufacturing practices and maintaining competitive advantage in the market.

data.title: CME Group to Launch 24/7 Cryptocurrency Futures and Options Trading
data.description: CME Group is set to introduce round-the-clock cryptocurrency futures and options trading starting May 29. According to Odaily, this move aims to enhance market accessibility and provide continuous trading opportunities for investors. The initiative reflects the growing demand for cryptocurrency trading and the need for flexible trading hours in the digital asset market.

data.title: UK Seeks Saudi Investment in Fighter Jet Program Amid Rising Costs
data.description: The United Kingdom is intensifying efforts to secure investment from Saudi Arabia for its next-generation fighter jet program, which is being developed in collaboration with Italy and Japan. Bloomberg posted on X that this move comes as the project faces escalating costs and a competing initiative experiences delays. The UK aims to bolster the program's financial backing and strengthen international partnerships in the defense sector. The collaboration with Saudi Arabia is seen as a strategic step to ensure the project's viability and success in the competitive global market.

data.title: Figure Launches FGRD Token with Instant Settlement and Lending Features
data.description: Figure has unveiled its FGRD token, representing common shares of the company directly issued on the blockchain. According to NS3.AI, this token provides instant settlement and incorporates built-in lending capabilities. To support this groundbreaking financial product, the company has expanded its funding round to $150 million.

data.title: Vale Sells Majority Stake in Canadian Nickel Venture
data.description: Vale has reached an agreement to sell the majority of its stake in a Canadian nickel venture to Exiro, Orion, and CGF. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting this move as part of Vale's strategic review of its base metals business. The decision aligns with the company's broader efforts to optimize its portfolio and focus on core operations.

data.title: The Chicago Mercantile Exchange Group will launch 24/7 cryptocurrency futures and options trading on May 29.
data.description: According to market sources, CME Group will launch 24/7 cryptocurrency futures and options trading on May 29.

data.title: Morgan Stanley Reduces Fees for Private Company Share Trading on EquityZen
data.description: Morgan Stanley has significantly lowered the fees for clients trading shares of private companies on its recently acquired EquityZen platform. Bloomberg posted on X that this move aims to attract more clients by offering competitive pricing, thereby challenging other firms in the market. The decision to cut costs by 50% is part of Morgan Stanley's strategy to expand its presence in the private equity trading sector. By reducing fees, the financial institution seeks to enhance its appeal to investors looking to trade private company shares, a market that has been gaining traction. This initiative reflects Morgan Stanley's commitment to providing cost-effective solutions and increasing its market share in the private equity space.

data.title: As Base migrates from the OP Stack to a proprietary technology stack, OP prices have fallen by over 20%.
data.description: The price of Optimism's native token, OP, has fallen by over 20% in the past 24 hours after Layer 2 solution Base announced its transition from the OP Stack to its self-running unified technology stack, base/base. Base stated that this move aims to accelerate development and reduce system complexity, and future node operators will need to migrate to the Base client to accommodate the hard fork. Base launched as the OP Stack chain in 2023, and according to a previous agreement, a portion of its sorter revenue was to be deposited into the Optimism treasury. With Base's shift to an independent architecture, this revenue stream will be retained by Base itself. Currently, OP is trading at $0.1436, a drop of approximately 97% from its all-time high of $4.84 in 2024. In contrast, Base has not yet issued any tokens.

data.title: The number of initial jobless claims in the U.S. this week was lower than expected.
data.description: The number of Americans filing for initial jobless claims for the week ending February 14 was 206,000, the lowest since the week ending January 10, compared to market expectations of 225,000. (Jinshi)

data.title: India and Netherlands to Collaborate on AI and Digital Advancements
data.description: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi met with the Dutch Prime Minister during the AI Impact Summit, discussing significant progress in digital and artificial intelligence sectors. According to Jin10, the two leaders agreed to collaborate in these fields, focusing on strengthening ties in semiconductors, major water projects, green hydrogen, talent mobility, and counter-terrorism efforts.

data.title: Excluding Noncitizens from Census Could Impact Diverse Cities
data.description: The U.S. Census aims to count every individual residing in the country. Bloomberg posted on X that excluding noncitizens from this count could significantly affect America's largest and most racially diverse urban areas. This exclusion could lead to an underrepresentation of these communities, impacting federal funding and political representation. The debate continues as stakeholders consider the implications of such a policy change.

data.title: AGRICULTURE | U.S. Farmers to Increase Soybean Plantings Amid Export Revival to China
data.description: U.S. farmers are set to increase soybean plantings this spring while reducing corn cultivation, according to recent reports. Bloomberg posted on X that this shift comes as soybean exports to China have resumed after a period of stagnation. The revival of these exports is influencing planting decisions, with growers responding to the renewed demand from one of the world's largest soybean importers. Corn, traditionally the most widely grown crop in the United States, will see a reduction in planting as farmers adjust to market dynamics. This change reflects broader trends in agricultural trade and the impact of international demand on domestic farming strategies.

data.title: Bank of England's Mann Signals Extended Interest Rate Hold Amid Inflation Concerns
data.description: Bank of England policymaker Catherine Mann has indicated her readiness to maintain interest rates at their current level for an extended period to manage inflation expectations. Bloomberg posted on X that Mann's stance comes despite her concerns about increasing unemployment rates. Mann emphasized the importance of controlling inflation to stabilize the economy, suggesting that a prolonged hold on interest rates might be necessary to achieve this goal. Her comments reflect the central bank's cautious approach in balancing economic growth with inflation control.
